observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct:
The GSO and the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in tracing the missing documents relevant to the unidentified PPE beginning balances to sort out the unreconciled differences between their respective records and ascertain the accuracy and existence of the PPE account;
The Inventory Committee to:
Prepare and submit to the Audit Team a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory activities.
Record/document the conduct of the physical count of PPEs with the Inventory Count Form (ICF), using the format in Annex A (COA Circular No. 2020-006, dated January 31, 2020); and
Properly prepare the RPCPPE with certification as to correctness by the Inventory Committee and approved by the Head of the Agency;
The Municipal Assessor to propose a budget allocation for the preparation of documentary requirements and the undertaking of the necessary courses of action to secure the Land Titles to protect the LGU from possible adverse claims of third parties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Draw the necessary journal entries to correct:
the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account in the General Fund by setting-up the transfer of the ₱993,209.35 reprogrammed amount from the Trust Fund to the General Fund; and
the erroneous transfer to the Trust Fund of the unexpended balance amounting to ₱634,722.69 of the CY 2022 DRRMF-capital outlay.
To correct the records of the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account in the General Ledger and the supporting details in the Subsidiary Ledgers for both the General Fund and the Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Make the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements;
Submit to the audit team the supporting documents of the recorded Accounts Payable; and
Verify the validity of the recorded claims in the prior years of the Accounts Payable and undertake the appropriate action as necessary.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant and the GSO conduct a periodic reconciliation of the Inventory account to ensure that the balance of the said account on their separate records is reconciled at year end; and
The Municipal Accountant makes the necessary journal entries to correct the balances of the Inventory and other affected accounts as of year end.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, on the disclosure of relevant information of the PPE account in the Notes to FS; and
Make the necessary corrections on the discrepancies and/or reporting errors in the amounts of certain PPE items as reported in the General Ledger and in the supporting Notes to FS (Note 28 - PPE).
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer make an entry to record the funds received from the DILG RO8, and update and balance daily her Cashbooks to ensure accuracy of financial records.

We recommended that the:
JO personnel to submit original copies of DTRs and accomplishment reports and original/authenticated copies of logbook/biometric printouts (whichever is applicable) as supporting documents for the payment of their wages and ensure that time entries on DTRs reconcile with the records of attendance as reflected on accomplishment reports.
LCE/HRMO provide a written justification for the 65 Job Order personnel that were absent during the headcount along with a valid document and identification proving their existence.
The foregoing recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension or Disallowance depending upon the LGU’s compliance with the audit recommendations and the results of the post-audit of the subject transactions.

We recommended that the:
Revenue Collectors strictly comply with the daily remittance of collections and in full amount to the Municipal Treasurer, and likewise, the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all her collections intact daily, or not later than the next banking day, or as required by the regulations;
Municipal Treasurer, stop the issuance of accountable forms to accountable officers who have pending unremitted collections; and
Revenue Collectors and the Municipal Treasurer properly record collections and remittances/deposits on the cashbooks in the manner they transpired; and provide the actual dates of deposit of collections on the cashbook consistent with the dates of validated deposit slips.

We recommended that the LCE directs the GAD Focal Person to ensure compliance with the 5% minimum budget allocation either as a separate GAD fund or by attributing the agency’s PAPs to the GAD budget or a combination of both.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that the GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report are submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period as required in Paragraph V of COA Circular No. 2014-001, together with the required supporting documents.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the understatement of the Accumulated Depreciation in previous years to be able to present the PPE in its proper valuation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant eliminate the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in the preparation of the Consolidated Financial Statements in consonance with accounting rules and regulations.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the proper recording of transactions in the Trust Fund books and the preparation of its financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entry to correct the year-end balance of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account and strictly observe the check disbursements process and reporting to establish the reliability of the account presentation.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant on the immediate reversal or restoration of the check that had been outstanding for more than six months to its proper Cash in Bank - Local Currency Current Account to establish the reliable and proper value of the account.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to return to the Trust Fund the amount of ₱1,861,288.88 which was erroneously drawn from the General Fund and for the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary journal entries accordingly.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on a regular basis pursuant to the pertinent provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MDRRM Officer to consider the entire amount allocated to the LDRRMF and to include all the unexpended prior years balance of the LDRRMF transferred to the Trust Liability – DRRM account in the preparation of its LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the accounting and reporting requirement of the LGU for the implementation of the projects/programs/activities under the LDRRMF as outlined in Item 5.1 of the COA Circular 2012-002 is faithfully adhered to by the personnel concerned. We also recommended that the MDRRMO and the Municipal Budget Officer reconcile the amounts in the RAAO and the Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the reporting requirement of the LGU for the implementation of the programs to be funded by LGSF-SBDP NTF ELCAC and LGSF GEF as outlined in Item 5.2 of the Local Budget Circular No. 144 and Local Budget Circular 146 is faithfully adhered to by the personnel concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger for each beneficiary barangay and projects to be implemented under the LGSF-SBDP NTF ELCAC to faithfully adhere to existing rules, regulations, and policies in keeping the records of the Municipality.

We recommended that the:

Local Chief Executive fill-up the vacancy with a qualified Municipal Assessor to fast-track the update of the assessment roll through a frequent physical survey. Provided, however, that the necessary technical and manpower support is provided to that Office; and

Municipal Treasurer strategizes and strengthens the collection drive beyond the required posting and sending out of notices to support and sustain the LGU’s goals and objectives in providing better services and facilities to its constituents

We recommended that the Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant comply with the required reconciliation to sort out the unreconciled differences of their respective records to ascertain the accuracy and existence of the PPE for reliable and useful information in the decision-making of the LGU

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately require the
Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to increase the Cash in
Bank account by the existing balance of the excluded bank account to fairly state
the same and achieve reliability of its presentation as purported qualitative
characteristics of financial reporting.

2.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Treasurer on the immediate accomplishment of the required IIRUP and
complete the disposal of those unserviceable properties in compliance with the
one-time cleansing of the account and obtain higher miscellaneous income
from the scrap value of those properties. Also, require the Municipal
Treasurer to tag the remaining serviceable properties of the LGU with stickers
of complete details. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to derecognize
the unserviceable properties only after its disposal to fairly state the PPE
account and afford the LGU of reliable and useful information in decision-
making.

3.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal
Accountant and the Inventory Committee to complete the reconciliation of
properties in to their respective records. Also, require the Municipal
Accountant to immediately book-up the unrecorded motor vehicles in the
books of accounts and in the appropriate property cards. Further, direct the
Inventory Committee to adhere the mechanisms provided in the Circular to
determine the cost/value of the acquired properties either through exchange
or non-exchange transactions with or without economic value to achieve a
fairly stated PPE account with complete details and accountabilities thereof
fully secured.

4.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive earnestly require the
Municipal Accountant to transfer the amount pooled for the KC- NCDDP-AF
Project to the Trust Fund books and open a separate depository account for the
specific purpose, if warranted, in compliance with the requirement of the MOA in
order to achieve the faithful presentation of the individual financial statements
for each fund.

5.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries to appropriately recognize the
expenditures in the TF and reimburse the cash balance in the GF thus reverse the
effect of erroneous charges and in order to maintain balanced budget of the LGU.

6.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to strictly adhere to the procedures in the preparation of the
SCBAA consistent and correspondent with the balances of the financial
statement components to establish reliable information and ascertain whether
the resources were used in accordance with the limits and bounds of the
approved budget of the LGU.

7.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal
Accountant to strictly follow the prescribed format and structure in the
preparation of the notes to financial statements in order to sustain the user’s
understanding of the report and attain reliability of its presentation taken as a
whole.

8.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to regularly prepare the required monthly bank reconciliation
statements for all the funds and bank accounts of the LGU for timely
adjustment and correction of the Cash in Bank account necessary and crucial
for relevant decision-making of the LGU Officials and other stakeholders.
Also, enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to come up with strategy for the timely
completion of their financial reports for submission to the Accounting Unit to
sustain the required timely preparation of BRS and correction of the Cash
account.

9.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Bids and Awards
Committee to consider proper planning and foresight in the procurement of
goods to promote economy wherein the most advantageous price for the
government is ensured.

10.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that only those well-
planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready projects shall be
considered in the recapitulation of the Development Projects in the Annual
Budget to afford the public with the benefits that can be derived thereat.

11.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall ensure that prescribed
timeline for budget preparation and submission be strictly observed for the on-
time enactment of the Annual Budget so that implementation of the projects
under the mandatory budgetary items such as the LDRRMF, specifically the
70% disaster prevention and mitigation fund, be attained within the fiscal year
that will reduce disaster risks and enhance disaster preparedness and response
capabilities of the local communities.

12.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly ensure that only
those allowable charges against the LDRRMF shall be approved so as to direct
the funds to its intended use and afford the community the benefits of disaster
risk prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and
recovery.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the Inventory Committee to:
Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) for the Mayor’s approval and submit the PIP to the Audit Team before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;
Start immediately, and complete during the year, the physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality, in accordance with the procedures and schedule in the PIP, with the assistance of the following officials:
Municipal Engineer – for the inventory of Buildings and other structures, Road Networks and other Infrastructure assets as well as the on-going projects at year-end;
Municipal Assessor - for the inventory of Land and Land Improvements
Prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCLRN, and submit the same to the Accountant for reconciliation and to the Audit Team for verification.
We also recommended that the Property Custodian prepare and maintain Property Cards for each class of PPE, and LRNPC for all roads and its components, based on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, respectively.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Update the PPELC for each class of PPE, and LRNLC for each local road, and reconcile these with the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, respectively; and
Start the cleansing of PPE accounts by following the procedures in COA Circular No. 2020-006 based on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant start the process for the filing of a request for write-off of dormant receivables to the Commission on Audit, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016,  after all remedies to collect the same have been exhausted.
We also strongly recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
stop processing Disbursement Vouchers for the grant of additional cash advances on the employees and officials with unsettled previous cash advances;
issue final demand letters for the settlement of long outstanding cash advances and impose the appropriate sanctions on those who fail to comply said demand.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer:
Obtain from DOH Regional Office, the actual information on the unit cost and quantities of the vaccines received by the LGU in relation to its vaccination program;
Provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete documents from the DOH relative to the receipt and distribution of the vaccines, at the end of each month.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the vaccines received by the LGU, based on the documents submitted by the MHO, as part of its inventory using the perpetual inventory system, and assist the Municipal Health Officer in the proper accounting of the vaccines received, as well as its distribution.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the members of the Municipal Planning Development Council:
Review the viability and necessity of unimplemented projects in the Annual Investment Plan funded from Current and Continuing Appropriations and rank said projects by order of priority, urgency and impact during the screening process of each budget year; and
Reprogram prior years’ projects to major projects that can be implemented within the specific time schedules during the budget year in order to maximize the utilization of the 20% DF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Punong Barangay and the Treasurer of the recipient barangays, in writing, to submit the Approved Program of Work and Financial Plan, as well as the Utilization Reports on the financial assistance granted to them by the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor include in the succeeding MOA for Fund Transfers to Implementing Barangays, provisions on the specific timeframe on the period of implementation of the project to be funded and the date of submission of the related Utilization Reports to ensure that the funds granted are promptly utilized and accounted for in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Furnish the Municipal Budget Office with a copy the LDRRM Fund Utilization Report for reconciliation and adjustment of the amount obligated in the RAO;
Transfer to the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund account the correct unexpended amount of the LDRRMF before the last working day of each year to be used for disaster management activities of the LGU within the next five years; and
Transfer to the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund the unexpended appropriation of CYs 2020 and 2021 totaling ₱1,101,476.05, after the enactment of an Ordinance approving its utilization.
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer:
Make the necessary adjustments in the RAO-LDRRMF at yearend based on the actual revenues collected and from the LDRRMF Utilization Report of the Municipal Accountant; and
Request for the enactment of an Ordinance to make available the unexpended appropriation of CYs 2020 and 2021 totaling ₱1,101,476.05, which were reverted to the surplus of the General Fund, PRIOR to payments in CY 2022.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor approves, and the Municipal Budget and Municipal Accountant process payments, for the utilization of the LDRRMF for PPAs which are included in the LDRRMFIP and integrated in the AIP; and are not administrative expenses.
We further recommended that in future transactions, the MDRRMC, through the MDRRMO, incorporate in the annual/revised LDRRMFIP and the Annual/Supplemental Investment Program, all PPAs to be charged to LDRRMF, including subsequent changes thereon, duly supported with proper authority through Resolutions from MDRRMC, MDC and the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, and other personnel concerned, to withhold and remit the appropriate amount of taxes from all payments to suppliers, including payments through reimbursements and cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and Municipal Accountant submit the following supporting documents to validate the eligibility and entitlement of the claimants, and to support the completeness, validity and authenticity of the payments.
Pre-determined list by the Local Health Board of the frontline HRHs and HWs, including the BHWs, duly validated by an authorized DOH representative;
Duly accomplished DTRs of all recipients of COVID-19 AHDP and SRA; and
Certification of the Municipal Health Officer that the claimants were assigned at the Rural Health Unit or in the LGU’s quarantine facility.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
deduct the amount disallowed from the salaries or other compensation of the incumbent persons liable;
require persons liable who are due to retire to settle their liabilities immediately prior to signing their clearances; and
impose other legal means to collect the disallowances from those who are no longer in the service of the LGU so as to return the funds to the bank account of the LGU for subsequent reappropriation.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make the appropriate adjusting entries in the books of accounts by recognizing the completion of the said Road Networks, and record its accumulated depreciation and depreciation expense accordingly.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record subsequent procurement of supplies and materials as an asset under the appropriate Inventory account upon purchase, and to recognize its related expenses only when supported with the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued from the end-users and Supply Officer with attached pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slips.

We also recommend that the Supply Officer prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued, within five days after the end of each month, supported with pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slips issued to end users as basis in recording the reduction of the Inventory account and its related expense account.

We recommend that the BAC Chairman, through its Secretariat, post in the PhilGEPS and GPPB website the Purchase Order, Contracts and other pertinent documents on the procurement activities of the Municipality of MacArthur.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure the BAC’s compliance with the on-line posting requirements in Republic Act No. 9184 and other relevant issuances.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should expedite the hiring of the said vacant positions, and solicit some advice from the CSC in order to facilitate its immediate hiring

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make the appropriate adjusting entries in the books of accounts by recognizing the completion of the said Road Networks, and record its accumulated depreciation and depreciation expense accordingly.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record subsequent procurement of supplies and materials as an asset under the appropriate Inventory account upon purchase, and to recognize its related expenses only when supported with the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued from the end-users and Supply Officer with attached pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slips.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to cancel the stale checks and restore the amount to the Cash in Bank-LCCA account and set-up the corresponding liability accounts after it has been identified and documented.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, together with the members of the Municipal Planning Development Council:
Update the Annual Investment Plan through a revisit of the unimplemented development projects and review its viability and necessity, taking into consideration and strictly observing the guidelines set in DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020, as well as other budgeting guidelines; and
Include in the Annual Investment Plan only those projects which can be implemented during the year in order to maximize the utilization of the 20% DF.

We recommend that the LDRRM Focal Person prepare the Supplemental LDRRMFIP for CY 2020 that will contain the Covid-19 related expenditures duly recommended by the LDRRMC and submit the same to the LDC for its inclusion in the Supplemental AIP duly ratified by the Sanggunian Bayan.

We recommend that the BAC Chairman, through its Secretariat, post in the PhilGEPS and GPPB website the Purchase Order, Contracts and other pertinent documents on the procurement activities of the Municipality of MacArthur.
We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure the BAC’s compliance with the on-line posting requirements in Republic Act No. 9184 and other relevant issuances.

We recommended management to:

Coordinate with agencies, accountable officers or operating units with unliquidated funds and require them to submit the liquidation documents in order to settle balances with the source agencies (SAs).

Verify the status of the transferred funds to identify unexpended/excess balances of projects already completed, for the return of the same to the SAs.

Prepare Statement and Aging of Due to NGAs to ensure proper monitoring of the funds transferred as well as status of projects being undertaken and furnish a copy to the Office of the Auditor.

a. The Municipal Treasurer, not to allow delayed or partial remittances of collections from RCCs.
b. The RCCs, through the Municipal Treasurer, to remit the collections in accordance with Section 8 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992 and other rules and regulations, as cited in this report.
c. The accountant, to see to it that all collections and remittances are recorded on appropriate time or year.

The Municipal Treasurer, and all accountable officers (AOs) concerned, to exercise due diligence in the collection, remittance, safekeeping and utilization of government funds, otherwise, impose sanctions against erring AOs

We highly recommend that:
a. The Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer should demand the accountable officers to produce the missing accountable form and submit an explanation/justificatio n for the absence of the lacking receipts.
b. The Accountable Officers should prepare the RAAF and the CRAAF within the reglementary period and reconcile its actual inventory of accountable forms against its records to ensure the completeness and safe keeping of the same in its custody, otherwise, impose sanctions if ever deemed necessary.
c. The MTO should likewise (a) segregate the recording of different accountable forms in its logbooks including the recording of all relevant information and (b) organize its records to enable all users of information to easily trace, monitor, and reconcile OR balances, so as to arrive at the correct balance at the end of each period and avoid further potential loss of ORs.

The Municipal Mayor should require all employees to adhere to provisions of RA 7160 and impose that claims and expenses under a certain year must all be obligated and paid by the budget allocated for that particular period and should not insist on the processing of claims submitted after the closing of the municipality’s books.

The Municipal Accountant should not accommodate the late submission and processing of claims after the date allotted for the same has lapsed, otherwise, share the fiscal responsibility arising from the improper claim along with all signatories which facilitated the release of funds.

All government employees should not be negligent in the submission of all documents to the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant for transactions before cut-off, otherwise, they forfeit the right to claim for remuneration and invalidate the lawful payment of expenditures incurred from the preceding period.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that:

The Municipal Mayor should require that all expenditures unduly paid by the LGU on behalf of the employees concerned be paid back in full and reverted to the funds on which payment was taken including the interest and penalties, or submit to the Office of the Auditor the documents which may prove otherwise.

The Municipal Mayor should require payroll personnel to update all employee records so as to come up with an accurate data as to salary deductions, remittances, and loan balances. The Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Treasurer to make sure that all deductions and payables entered into by LGU employees which have fallen due and demandable be properly deducted in the respective salaries and strictly require the Accountable Officer to remit “all” due premium and loan remittances for any given month, as well as require all concerned employees to pay the proper amount required in their monthly dues, otherwise, they must personally shoulder the interests and penalties arising from the late payment of obligations.

The Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Office of the Auditor all complete and accurate documents and billings downloaded/acquired by the municipality from the GSIS, LBP, and DBP for at least the past 3 years especially those documents which were the basis for the balances of the variances reported by the Municipal Treasurer on the remittance of GSIS Premiums including the MOAs entered into by the LGU and the LBP, DBP and GSIS in regards to personnel loans.

The Municipal Mayor along with the other concerned officials should be vigilant in the granting of additional loans and all signatories should give emphasis on the prevention of additional loans which may contradict GAA 2017 provisions which requires employees’ Net Take Home Pay to be not lower than P4,000.00.

All employees concerned should update their loans and premium payments to make sure that all payments deducted from their salaries have been duly remitted and pay back all payments improperly made by the LGU on their behalf.

The Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to update and adjust the LDRRMF balance in the Financial Statement. Record all transactions charged under the fund and coordinate with the LDRRMO to reconcile their balances and arrive at the correct valuation of the unutilized LDRRMF onwards so as to avoid the variances on the reports generated by the LGU causing confusion to the various users of the LGU’s information.

The Municipal Accountant should record the appropriate adjusting journal entries to transfer the unutilized balances of the current and previous years’ LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund using the “Trust Liability-DRRM” account.

The Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO should coordinate on a regular basis to continuously monitor and strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 and other Relevant Implementing Rules and Regulations.

The Municipal Mayor should remind the Municipal Accountant and all other personnel to ensure the Accuracy and Completeness of the Financial and all other reports and records before the submission of the same to the various Government Agencies and its publication.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Municipal Treasurer, not to allow delayed or partial remittances of collections from RCCs.
The RCCs, through the Municipal Treasurer, to remit the collections in accordance with Section 8 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992 and other rules and regulations, as cited in this report.
The accountant, to see to it that all collections and remittances are recorded on appropriate time or year.
The Municipal Treasurer, and all accountable officers (AOs) concerned, to exercise due diligence in the collection, remittance, safekeeping and utilization of government funds, otherwise, impose sanctions against erring AOs

We highly recommended that:
a. The Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer should demand the accountable officers to produce the missing accountable form and submit an explanation/justification for the absence of the lacking receipts.
b. The Accountable Officers should prepare the RAAF and the CRAAF within the reglementary period and reconcile its actual inventory of accountable forms against its records to ensure the completeness and safe keeping of the same in its custody, otherwise, impose sanctions if ever deemed necessary.
c. The MTO should likewise (a) segregate the recording of different accountable forms in its logbooks including the recording of all relevant information and (b) organize its records to enable all users of information to easily trace, monitor, and reconcile OR balances, so as to arrive at the correct balance at the end of each period and avoid further potential loss of ORs.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant not to accommodate and process documents of claims which were submitted after the lapse of allotted time, otherwise, share the fiscal responsibility arising from the illegal payment thereof.
The Municipal Mayor should also require all employees to adhere to provisions of RA 7160 and impose that claims and expenses under a certain year must all be obligated and paid by the budget allocated for that particular period and should not insist on the processing of claims submitted after the closing of the municipality’s books.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor should require that all expenditures unduly paid by the LGU in behalf of the employees concerned be paid back in full and reverted to the funds on which payment was taken including the interest and penalties, or submit to the Office of the Auditor the documents which may prove otherwise.
The Municipal Mayor should require payroll personnel to update all employee records so as to come up with an accurate data as to salary deductions, remittances, and loan balances. The Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Treasurer to make sure that all deductions and payables entered into by LGU employees which have fallen due and demandable be properly deducted in the respective salaries and strictly require the Accountable Officer to remit “all” due premium and loan remittances for any given month, as well as require all concerned employees to pay the proper amount required in their monthly dues, otherwise, they must personally  shoulder the interests and penalties arising from the late payment of obligations.
The Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Office of the Auditor all complete and accurate documents and billings downloaded/acquired by the municipality from the GSIS, LBP, and DBP for at least the past 3 years especially those documents which were the basis for the balances of the variances reported by the Municipal Treasurer on the remittance of GSIS Premiums including the MOAs entered into by the LGU and the LBP, DBP and GSIS with regard to personnel loans.
The Municipal Mayor along with the other officials concerned should be vigilant in the granting of additional loans and all signatories should give emphasis on the prevention of additional loans which may contradict GAA 2017 provisions which requires employees’ Net Take Home Pay to be not lower than P4,000.00.
All employees concerned should update their loans and premium payments to make sure that all payments deducted from their salaries have been duly remitted and pay back all payments improperly made by the LGU on their behalf.
We recommended that management appropriate 1% of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for programs, projects and activities of the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC).

We recommend that the Inventory Committee be required to conduct a complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality so that it can prepare and submit the required physical inventory reports to the Auditor and thereafter, regularly submit such reports to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.  Likewise, in the process of the conduct of physical inventory, verify if such non-moving account balances are still existing, and make the proper adjusting entries on such accounts, if warranted.  We also recommend the account Hostels and Dormitories should be reclassified to the Other Structures account.
We further recommend that the PARs of such properties be renewed periodically every three (3) years.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a journal entry voucher to take up the adjustment by debiting the Prior Year’s Adjustment, since the income accounts for 2017 are now closed, and credit Due to NGAs account, both in the amount of P12,946,000.00.  Accordingly, the Municipal Accountant is also directed to orient and familiarize herself with the prescribed chart of accounts, particularly on the description of the account titles.

We recommend that management file the request for authority to write-off dormant/ accounts totaling P1,873,421.70 to the COA Audit Team pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

We recommend that the Municipal Assessor be directed to prioritize the titling of those untitled lands in order to establish absolute ownership thereof, and for the Municipal Budget Officer to consider the funding requirement for the titling of lands in the budget preparation for the ensuing year.

We recommend that the Municipal Assessor provide the Municipal Treasurer the assessment roll as basis for the Municipal Treasurer in collecting the real property tax.  Accordingly, we also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer update its real property tax account register, and render collection notices to the concerned taxpayers.  Likewise, provide the Municipal Accountant a certified list of the taxpayers, as well as its total amount due and collectible for the year 2017, and thereafter, provide such duly certified list at the beginning of every year.  Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant record immediately the receivables as soon as the Municipal Treasurer provide such list.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) be required to strictly observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided for in DILG-DBM JMC no. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  Likewise, maximize the utilization of appropriated fund in implementing the development projects indicated in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommend that the SEF Budget be prepared by the District Supervisor and approved by the Local School Board (LSB) and duly supported with programs, projects, and activities that are aligned with Section 100(b) of RA 7160, and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017. Limit the use of SEF for purposes for which it was intended. Likewise, see to it that the procurement documents are properly accomplished to ascertain the legality, propriety and validity of government transactions.

We recommend that management submit at the beginning of the year a list of  on-going and to be implemented programs/projects/activities (PPAs)  for the current year, and inform COA within ten (10) days after the award of the projects or before the start of the PPAs that the appropriate project  signboards,   blackboards, whiteboards, posters, tarpaulins streamers, electronic boards, or similar materials (collectively, “Signboards”) not exceeding 3 feet by  2 feet posted for notification as  public notices  as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should expedite the hiring of the said vacant positions, and solicit some advice from the CSC in order to facilitate its immediate hiring.

The local Chief Executive should create and Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. Then, the Municipal Treasurer shall prepare and update the Property Cards, while the Municipal Accountant shall update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Real Property Ledger Cards. With these, regular reconciliation of accounting and property records could be undertaken. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. The Municipal Treasurer should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU will be completely/accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties could be properly accounted, disposed and recorded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Budget Officer to submit a reconciliation of the two financial
reports and/or submit justifications on the discrepancy.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and
supporting documents, Report of Checks Issued and other necessary documents
to the Auditor completely within the regulatory period and adhere strictly to the
prescribed accounting and auditing rules and regulations to give the users
correct and relevant data at any given time. Otherwise, impose the
withholding of salaries and allowances of concerned personnel for continuous
failure to comply with Section 122 of PD 1445.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Mayor direct the Collecting Officers to immediately remit at
the closing of the day or the day immediately following the date of collection.
All collections and remittances be properly recorded in the books and
promptly deposited to the bank within the period legally stated so as to avoid
potential loss and/or inappropriate usage of government funds;
b. Adhere to governing rules and regulations as stated above and to be strict as
to the implementation of controls for cash and its equivalents including the
implementation of proper sanctions as provided by related IRRs should the
need for drastic actions arise;
c. Avoid the accumulation of transactions by observing proper cut-off in the
reporting of collections and the immediate remittance thereafter;
d. Demand proper review and verification by responsible officers (Liquidating
Officer/Municipal Treasurer) of the Report of Collections and Deposits
submitted by the RCCs.
e. The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit regularly to the Audit Team
the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms With and
Without Money Value.
f. Strictly adhere to the provision of Section 73 of GAAM, Volume 1 and issue
official receipts following the correct and appropriate numerical sequence so
as to avoid the risk of malversation.

We recommend to management to ensure that all claims shall be supported
with the required documents to conform with Section 4, par. 6 of PD 1445 and
Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
submit the necessary requirements for liquidation to the Office of the Chairman
of the Commission on Audit and adhere to provisions of COA Circular No. 2003-
003 - providing the general guidelines in the disbursement of Intelligence Funds.
Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer stop the charging of
disbursements for Intelligence/Confidential expenditures under the 20%
Development Fund.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct all employees to comply
strictly with Section 52 of RA No. 10717 or the General Appropriations Act
(GAA) for Fiscal Year 2016 and that they would not act on additional loan
applications unless the P3,500.00 net take home pay is satisfied.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWDO and the
Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking documentary
requirements required under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 04 s. 2015 and
avoid future grants before necessary supporting information have been
established so as to avoid suspensions and disallowances.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer
and the Municipal Accountant to submit additional documents if the
disbursements were made with sufficient appropriation and/or submit written
justifications on the deficiency.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant,
Municipal Treasurer, and all officers responsible for the preparation of
Disbursement Vouchers and Liquidation Reports to:
a. Strictly adhere to provisions of COA Circulars 2012-001 and 97-002 for the
granting and liquidation of cash advances;
b. Strictly adhere to provisions of R.A. 9184 and avoid procurement thru
reimbursement basis.

We recommend that:
a. The BAC Chairman submit to the audit team all perfected contracts during
the year; and,
b. Strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the
submission of contracts for auditorial review.

We recommend that:
a. The BAC verify, validate and ascertain that all statements made in the
contract and documents submitted with it adheres to the guidelines in the
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184.
b. The Head of Procuring Entity enters into contract with the winning bidder
only if all the documentary requirements are complied with.

We recommend that:
 a. The Municipal Accountant-Designate and the ICO-Municipal
Treasurer immediately transfer the unexpended/unobligated balances
of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE in CY 2016 totaling P1,931,590.00
from the General Fund to Trust Fund Books.
 b. Henceforth, the ICO-Municipal Treasurer, LDRRMO and the
Municipal Accountant-Designate coordinate with each other in
updating monthly the LDRRMF utilization in order to determine and
transfer the unexpended/unobligated balances of QRF and DRRMF-
MOOE from the General Fund to the Trust Fund Books on or before
the last banking day of the current year.

We recommend that:
 a. The Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting journal entries to
correct the books of accounts.
 b. The Municipal Accountant review the accounting entries prepared by
her staff using as guide the Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009
dated December 1, 2015 and other related issuances, and ensure that
the accounting entries are supported with bank debit memo, bank
statements and other pertinent supporting documents.
 c. The Municipal Accountant exert more effort to supervise her staff and
require the Municipal Bookkeeper to report to work regularly and
perform the bookkeeping functions.

We recommend that:
 a. The Municipal Accountant-Designate familiarize with the Revised
Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
 b. The Municipal Accountant-Designate review the posting from journals to
the books of accounts and other accounting works of her staff.
 c. Management require the Municipal Bookkeeper to report to work
regularly and discharge his functions and perform the bookkeeping
functions.
 d. Management assign additional personnel with accounting educational
background to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommend that:
 a. The Municipal Accountant-Designate coordinate with the former
Municipal Accountant, in tracing the original accounting entries
made and if warranted, prepare the necessary adjusting journal
entries, to de-recognize the accounts from the books.
 b. Management, through the Municipal Accountant, strictly adhere
with the provisions of Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommend that:
 a. The Municipal Accountant-Designate prepare the proper adjusting
journal entries to correct the books of accounts.
 b. The Municipal Accountant-Designate review the accounting entries
prepared by her staff using as guide the Annex B of COA Circular No.
2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and other related issuances, and
ensure that the accounting entries are supported with bank debit
memo, bank statements and other pertinent supporting documents.
 c. Management require the Municipal Bookkeeper to report to work
regularly and perform the bookkeeping functions.
 d. Management fill up the vacant positions in the Office of the Municipal
Accountant.
 e. Management assign additional personnel with accounting educational
background to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommend that:
 a. The Municipal Accountant prepare the proper accounting entries to
adjust the books of accounts.
 b. The Municipal Accountant review the accounting entries prepared
by her staff using as guide the Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-
009 dated December 1, 2015 and other related issuances.
 c. Management require the Municipal Bookkeeper to report to work
regularly and perform the bookkeeping functions.
 d. Management assign additional personnel with accounting
educational background to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommend that:
 a. The Inventory Committee, General Services Officer, Municipal
Engineer and Municipal Accountant-Designate comply with the
Accounting and Reporting Procedures enumerated in Item V of
COA Circular N0. 2015-008.
 b. The General Services Officer maintain a Local Road Inventory and
Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card
for all roads and its components.
 c. The Municipal Engineer provide the local accountant and the
general services officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.
 d. The Local Accountant
 d.1 Prepare the Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) in
 accordance with Item IX of COA Circular No. 2015-008, and to
 maintain the LRNLC for each local road;
 d.2 Prepare a Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance
 of the local road network and its components in the general
 ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC);
 d.3 Support the local road network and its components with the
 Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads and working paper
 on the distribution of costs for the road components; and
 d.4 Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road
 components for every road network.

We recommend that henceforth, the Inventory Committee undertake an
annual inventory of all insurable property of the agency and submit the report to
GSIS and furnish a copy to the COA Auditor on or before October 31 of each
ensuing year.

We recommend that:
 a. Management, through the ICO-Municipal Treasurer, General
Services Officer, and LDRRMC insure yearly with the GSIS the
LGU’s insurable properties based on the annual inventory of
insurable properties, using the unexpended/unobligated QRF and
DRRMF-MOOE funds.
 b. Management appropriate in its budget for the payment of
premiums for adequate insurance coverage with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS of the LGU’s insurable assets.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the ceiling set in the
DBM National Budget Circulars and other related rules and regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Account require the submission of
Official Receipts issued by taxi operators instead of RER for claims for taxi
fares.

We recommend that:
 c. Management pay the salaries, wages, and other monetary benefits of
the agency’s elective officials, regular, casual, contractual and job
order personnel through Automated Teller Machine instead of cash.
 d. Management coordinate with nearby agencies and with the AGDB for
the installation of ATMs nearby.

We recommend that:
 a. Henceforth, management strictly adhere to the provisions of the
annual General Appropriations Act on the minimum monthly net
take-home pay.
 b. Require the concerned officials involved in approving personal loans
to control the loan amount that an employee could avail of by
considering the net monthly net take-home pay.
 c. Require the personnel in charge of preparing the payrolls and
disbursement vouchers for the payment of claims for Personnel
Services to use as guide the provisions of the annual General
Appropriations Act in prioritizing the authorized deductions that can
be charged against the employees’ claims for Personnel Services,
subject to the monthly net take-home pay limitation.
 d. Management, through the Office of the Municipal Accountant, inform
in writing the concerned employees on authorized and other
deductions that can no longer be deducted from their salaries due to
monthly net take-home pay limitation.

We recommend that:
 a. The ICO-Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all collections to
authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking
day.
 b. Management assign additional personnel to the Office of the ICO-
Municipal Treasurer.

We recommend that the Collectors/Tellers turn over their collections daily
to the Liquidating Officer in strict adherence to Sections 60(A) and 61(B) Of the
Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommend that:
 a. The Municipal Accountant-Designate update the subsidiary
accounting records and furnish the ICO-Municipal Treasurer with
a certified copy thereof at the end of each month.
 b. The ICO-Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balances with
the subsidiary accounting records every end of the month in strict
adherence to the provisions of Section 63 (F) of the Local Treasury
Operations Manual.
 c. The Municipal Accountant-Designate and the ICO-Municipal
Treasurer submit to the Auditor the Reconciliation Statements
monthly.

We recommend that:
 a. The ICO-Municipal Treasurer submit weekly the financial transaction
documents and reports to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
 b. The Municipal Accountant-Designate immediately record the
submitted reports and supporting documents in the books of accounts
and submit the financial transaction documents within the first ten
(10) days of the ensuing month.
 c. Management assign additional personnel to the Offices of the ICO-
Municipal Treasurer and Accountant.
 d. The Municipal Bookkeeper report to the Accounting Office and
discharge his duties accordingly.

d. The GAD Focal person c submit to the Auditor the GAD Accomplishment
Report for CY 2016 using the prescribed format.

e. Henceforth, the GAD Focal Person submit the DILG- approved GAD
Plan and Budget for the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report
monthly using the format prescribed in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 and other related
rules and regulations.

f. Henceforth, the GAD Focal Person, the Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator, and the Municipal Accountant meet monthly in order to
update the GAD Fund utilization and accomplishment and for the
Municipal Accountant to submit to the Auditor the schedule of the
corresponding check disbursement details.

The local Chief Executive should create Property, and Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. Then, the Municipal Treasurer shall prepare and update the Property Cards, while the Municipal Accountant shall update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Real Property Ledger Cards. With these, regular reconciliation of accounting and property records could be undertaken. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. The Municipal Treasurer should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU will be completely/accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties could be properly accounted, disposed and recorded.
